name: Daily tadalafil increased abdominal lean mass in mild ED/LUTS but did not test strength or exercise performance
statement: In nonobese men with mild ED and/or LUTS, tadalafil 5 mg once daily for 2 months increased abdominal lean mass, which returned to baseline after withdrawal, but the study did not establish improved strength, hypertrophy from training, or exercise performance.
population_or_context: Nonobese men with mild ED and/or LUTS, mean age about 48.5 years.
time_horizon: Two months plus two-month withdrawal observation.
quantitative_note: Once-daily tadalafil increased abdominal lean mass (P<0.01) and improved endothelial function (P<0.05); abstract reports return of lean mass to baseline after withdrawal.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports randomization, DXA body composition outcome, endothelial/hormone changes, and conclusion.

caveats: No placebo arm reported in abstract.
Body composition is not strength or exercise capacity.
population_profile: Nonobese symptomatic men near the target age band, not mostly healthy performance users.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily versus 20 mg on demand for 2 months.
indication_context: ED/LUTS clinical study with body-composition and mechanistic outcomes.
comparator_context: On-demand tadalafil comparator and withdrawal baseline.
endpoint_measure: DXA abdominal lean mass, ED/LUTS questionnaires, hormones, endothelial function, skeletal muscle cell myogenin/androgen receptor expression.
effect_estimate: Abdominal lean mass increased with once-daily tadalafil but returned to baseline after withdrawal; functional performance was not measured.
safety_findings: Detailed adverse events not extracted from abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for men age 30 to 50 with mild ED/LUTS; low for healthy exercise performance or strength claims.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Symptom-selected population, no placebo arm in abstract, active-dose comparison, and no functional endpoint.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but body-composition signals cannot be converted into performance or longevity claims.
